tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the documentation to understand ClubDoorman's purpose: a Telegram bot for automated club moderation with AI-powered spam detection, captcha verification, and admin controls. Read the approval system docs, media filtering guide, and log chat setup instructions.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: Learn how the bot boots up: Program.cs bootstraps the .NET Host, registers all services via DI, and configures the Telegram bot. Worker.cs is the background service that processes incoming Telegram updates in a loop.

tour.title: Service Registration & DI
tour.description: Understand how all services are wired together. ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs is the central DI registration hub. Also review infrastructure utilities like Consts, ChatSettingsManager, and the OpenAI extensions.

tour.title: Feature Modules
tour.description: Explore the three core feature modules that use a facade pattern to encapsulate complex workflows: AdminOps (admin commands dashboard), Moderation (spam detection and effects building), and UserJoin (user join flow with captchas and welcome messages).

tour.title: Effect System
tour.description: Study the moderation effects system: EffectBus orchestrates action execution, Effects.cs defines the effect types, and individual effect classes (BanUserEffect, DeleteToLogEffect, ReportMessageEffect, RequireAiAnalysisEffect, RequireManualReviewEffect) implement specific moderation actions.

tour.title: Core Services
tour.description: Dive into the domain services: AI pipeline (AiCascadeService, SpamHamClassifier, MimicryClassifier), captcha verification, message handling pipeline with its steps, notification system, statistics tracking, and the update dispatcher that routes Telegram events.

tour.title: Data Models
tour.description: Review the domain models and DTOs: ModerationResult for action outcomes, CaptchaInfo/CaptchaResult for verification flow, notification types (AdminNotificationType, LogNotificationType, UserNotificationType), request models for captcha and messaging, and logging models (ModerationEvent, RuleCode).

tour.title: Configuration & Deployment
tour.description: Review project configuration: appsettings.json for production settings, appsettings.Development.json for local overrides, ClubDoorman.csproj for dependencies and build config, Dockerfile for containerization, and env.example for required environment variables.
